name: 厦门
description: Xiamen, a coastal city in Fujian Province, is known as the '海上花园' (Garden on the Sea). With its blend of colonial architecture, traditional Minnan culture, and modern development, it offers a unique mix of East and West. The city features beautiful beaches, the UNESCO-listed Gulangyu Island, Buddhist temples, and world-class cuisine.
highlights: Gulangyu Island - UNESCO World Heritage colonial island
Nanputuo Temple - Sacred Buddhist mountain temple
Xiamen University - China's most beautiful campus

climate.tips: Best visited in spring (March-May) or autumn (October-November) for pleasant weather. Summers are hot and humid with frequent typhoons. Winters are mild but can be rainy. Bring rain gear year-round and sunscreen in summer.

attractions.name: 鼓浪屿
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage island known for its colonial architecture, piano museum, and car-free streets. Features over 1,000 historical buildings and stunning ocean views.
attractions.tips: Book ferry tickets in advance during holidays. Rent an audio guide at the pier. Best explored on foot - no cars allowed. Visit in early morning for fewer crowds.

attractions.name: 南普陀寺
attractions.description: One of the most important Buddhist temples in Fujian, set against the backdrop of Five Old Men Peak. Known for vegetarian restaurant and beautiful architecture.
attractions.tips: Climb the mountain for panoramic city views. Don't miss the vegetarian noodles at the temple restaurant. Respect Buddhist customs - dress modestly.

attractions.name: 厦门大学
attractions.description: Known as China's most beautiful university campus, featuring unique闽南建筑 (Minnan architecture), lakes, and palm-lined streets. Founded by Tan Kah Kee in 1921.
attractions.openingHours: Campus: Open 24 hours (ID required), Main Buildings: 08:00-17:00
attractions.tips: Book through XMU service app. Visit Furong Lake and the tunnel with student murals. The campus is stunning at sunset. Nearby Zengcuo'an for snacks.

attractions.name: 环岛路
attractions.description: A scenic 31km coastal road offering beautiful sea views, beaches, and parks. Perfect for cycling, jogging, or leisurely drives with stops at various beach areas.
attractions.address: Coastal road from Huli District to Xiang'an District
attractions.tips: Rent a bicycle at many rental points. Best visited at sunset. Combine with nearby attractions. Morning runs are popular with locals.

attractions.name: 中山路步行街
attractions.description: Xiamen's premier shopping street featuring arcade buildings with diverse architectural styles, local snacks, and international brands. The heart of downtown Xiamen.
attractions.tips: Explore side alleys for hidden temples and old houses. Try local snacks along the street. Best evening visit when buildings are illuminated.

attractions.name: 集美学村
attractions.description: A unique educational complex founded by philanthropist Tan Kah Kee, featuring Austro-Hungarian architecture, beautiful gardens, and several colleges.
attractions.tips: Combine with Huandao Road cycling. Visit the turtle-shaped Jimei Middle School. The illuminated buildings at night are magical.

attractions.name: 沙坡尾
attractions.description: The oldest fishing harbor in Xiamen, now transformed into a trendy art and dining district while retaining its traditional fishing village character.
attractions.tips: Best during blue hour for photos. Explore the art spaces and galleries. Fresh seafood available from local vendors. Evening atmosphere is fantastic.

attractions.name: 曾厝垵
attractions.description: A former fishing village turned bustling tourist town famous for its maze-like alleys, local snacks, handcraft shops, and vibrant nightlife.
attractions.tips: Get lost in the alleys intentionally. Try local snacks like土笋冻 and沙茶面. Night market atmosphere after sunset. Can be very crowded during holidays.

attractions.name: 厦门海底世界
attractions.description: One of the largest underwater exhibitions in Asia, featuring a 15-meter underwater tunnel and diverse marine life including sharks, sea turtles, and colorful fish.
attractions.tips: Go early to avoid school groups. Allow time for dolphin show schedule. Combine with other Gulangyu attractions. Air-conditioned - good escape from summer heat.

attractions.name: 钢琴博物馆
attractions.description: Houses over 40 antique pianos from different eras, including a rare 1796 grand piano. Located in Shuzhuang Garden on Gulangyu Island.
attractions.tips: Check timing for automatic piano performances. Photography allowed without flash. Combine with Shuzhuang Garden visit. Audio guide recommended.

attractions.name: 日光岩
attractions.description: The highest point on Gulangyu Island offering 360-degree panoramic views of Xiamen Bay and the surrounding area. Features ancient rock inscriptions and a small temple.
attractions.tips: Go at sunrise for fewer crowds and magical views. Climbed 100 steps - wear comfortable shoes. Morning visit recommended to avoid afternoon heat.

attractions.name: 菽庄花园
attractions.description: A beautiful seaside garden featuring traditional Chinese architecture, a piano museum, and stunning coastal views. Built in 1913 by wealthy Taiwanese businessman.
attractions.tips: Best photography in morning light. The 42-bridge path is unique. Combine with Sunlight Rock for full experience. Beautiful at high tide.

attractions.name: 海沧湾公园
attractions.description: A waterfront park along Haicang Bay featuring the iconic marriage witness sculpture, sunset views, and pleasant walking paths.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset for beautiful views. Evening light show at 19:30. Less touristy than other spots. Great for evening strolls.

attractions.name: 天竺山森林公园
attractions.description: A pristine forest park with hiking trails, waterfalls, and Buddhist temples. Known for its oxygen-rich environment and scenic mountain scenery.
attractions.tips: Wear hiking shoes for forest trails. Bring water and snacks. Cable car offers great views. Best in spring for flowers or autumn for foliage.

attractions.name: 厦门游乐园
attractions.description: A family-friendly amusement park with roller coasters, bumper cars, and various rides. Features both thrill rides and children's attractions.
attractions.tips: Go on weekdays to avoid crowds. Fast pass available. Combined ticket better value. Great for families with children.

attractions.name: 厦门园林植物园
attractions.description: A vast botanical garden featuring tropical plants, succulent collections, and famous rock formations. Known for the tropical rain forest trail.
attractions.tips: Start early to experience misty rainforest atmosphere. Take the观光车 (tour bus) to save time. Insect repellent recommended. Bring water.

attractions.name: 大嶝岛
attractions.description: A small island known for its traditional闽南 customs, coastal scenery, and historical sites related to the 1949 bombardment. Connected to mainland by bridge.
attractions.tips: Rent electric cart to explore. Fresh seafood at local restaurants. Combine with Xiang'an for day trip. Traditional architecture preserved.

attractions.name: 福建妈祖庙
attractions.description: The largest Mazu temple in Fujian, dedicated to the sea goddess worshipped by millions of overseas Chinese. Features intricate carvings and cultural exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Learn about Mazu culture and folklore. Attend temple ceremonies if lucky. Near Zhongshan Road. The architecture is impressive.

attractions.name: 火烧屿
attractions.description: An uninhabited island nature reserve known for its unique geological formations, hiking trails, and bird watching opportunities.
attractions.tips: Book boat tour in advance. Bring sunscreen and water. Limited facilities on island. Best for nature lovers and photographers.

attractions.name: 厦门科技馆
attractions.description: A modern science museum with interactive exhibits covering physics, astronomy, and technology. Features an IMAX dome theater and hands-on experiments.
attractions.tips: Allow full day for all attractions. Book IMAX tickets separately. Great for families and curious minds. Air-conditioned.

attractions.name: 厦门高尔夫球场
attractions.description: An 18-hole championship golf course near the coast, offering beautiful sea views and professional facilities for golf enthusiasts.
attractions.tips: Book tee times in advance. Non-members welcome. Golf equipment rental available. Beautiful at sunset.

attractions.name: 胡里山炮台
attractions.description: Historical fort built in 1894 during Qing Dynasty, featuring the world's largest surviving coastal artillery gun. Regular cannon firing demonstrations.
attractions.tips: Watch the cannon firing demonstration at scheduled times. Combine with nearby beaches. Great for history enthusiasts.

attractions.name: 厦门岛内山
attractions.description: A hidden gem mountain trail with panoramic views of Xiamen Bay and Gulangyu Island. Less crowded than other attractions.
attractions.tips: Best visited at dawn for sunrise. Wear comfortable hiking shoes. Bring water. Less known by tourists.

attractions.name: 厦门园林博览苑
attractions.description: A vast park featuring traditional Chinese gardens from different regions, plus a large lake and beautiful floral displays.
attractions.tips: Best in spring for flowers. Take the boat for scenic views. Can spend half a day here. Great for photography.

attractions.name: 厦门芙蓉隧道
attractions.description: Famous student art tunnel at Xiamen University, featuring colorful murals and graffiti created by students over the years.
attractions.tips: Combine with XMU campus visit. Don't touch or damage the artwork. Best visited on foot.

attractions.name: 鼓浪屿
attractions.description: 世界文化遗产，有万国建筑和钢琴之岛之称。
attractions.tips: 必去鼓浪屿。

attractions.name: 厦门大学
attractions.description: 中国最美丽的大学之一，有芙蓉湖和芙蓉隧道。
attractions.tips: 了解厦门教育。

attractions.name: 中山路
attractions.description: 厦门最古老的商业骑楼街。
attractions.tips: 品尝厦门小吃。

attractions.name: 南普陀寺
attractions.description: 闽南佛教圣地，有千年历史。
attractions.tips: 品尝素斋。

attractions.name: 环岛路
attractions.description: 厦门最美的海滨大道，全长43公里。
attractions.tips: 骑行看海。

attractions.name: 厦门美食
attractions.description: 厦门有沙茶面和各种海鲜小吃。
attractions.tips: 品尝厦门美食。

attractions.name: 厦门博物馆
attractions.description: 展示厦门历史的博物馆。
attractions.tips: 了解厦门文化。

attractions.name: 万石植物园
attractions.description: 厦门最大的植物园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 厦门夜景
attractions.description: 鼓浪屿和环岛路的夜景。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 白城沙滩
attractions.description: 厦门最美的海滩之一。
attractions.tips: 夏季最佳。

attractions.name: 曾厝垵
attractions.description: 厦门最文艺的渔村，有各种小吃和手工艺品。
attractions.tips: 感受厦门文艺。

attractions.name: 海沧大桥
attractions.description: 横跨海峡的大桥。
attractions.tips: 看海景。

attractions.name: 白鹭洲公园
attractions.description: 厦门最大的城市公园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 开元寺
attractions.description: 厦门最古老的佛教寺院。
attractions.tips: 了解宗教文化。

attractions.name: 八市
attractions.description: 厦门最古老的菜市场，有各种海鲜和小吃。
attractions.tips: 体验厦门生活。

attractions.name: 五老峰
attractions.description: 南普陀寺后面的山峰。
attractions.tips: 登高望远。

attractions.name: 厦门美术馆
attractions.description: 展示中外美术作品。
attractions.tips: 了解艺术文化。

attractions.name: 五缘湾
attractions.description: 厦门新开发的海湾。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 集美学村
attractions.description: 陈嘉庚创办的学校群，有嘉庚建筑风格。
attractions.tips: 了解陈嘉庚文化。

attractions.name: 永定土楼
attractions.description: 世界文化遗产，有独特的圆形土楼。
attractions.tips: 了解客家文化。

attractions.name: 厦门科技馆
attractions.description: 展示科技知识的博物馆。
attractions.tips: 适合亲子游。

attractions.name: 海湾公园
attractions.description: 厦门最大的海滨公园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 五通灯塔
attractions.description: 厦门东部的灯塔。
attractions.tips: 看海景。

attractions.name: 厦门老剧场文化中心
attractions.description: 厦门最古老的剧场。
attractions.tips: 了解厦门文化。

attractions.name: 白鹭洲公园
attractions.description: 厦门最大的城市公园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

restaurants.name: xm-hongxia

restaurants.name: xm-xianlu

restaurants.name: xm-xinyuan

restaurants.name: xm-longde

restaurants.name: xm-chengyi

restaurants.name: xm-furong

restaurants.name: xm-sanyu
restaurants.address: Near Zengcuo'an
restaurants.description: Popular late-night BBQ spot near Zengcuo'an, known for grilled seafood and skewers.

restaurants.name: xm-baocheng
restaurants.address: Jimei School Village

restaurants.name: xm-shengsheng

arrival.from: Major international airports (Singapore, Tokyo, Seoul, Bangkok)
arrival.duration: 3-6 hours from Asia, 8-12 hours from Europe/Americas
arrival.frequency: Multiple daily international flights, extensive domestic network
arrival.tips: Gaoqi Airport is close to city center (15 minutes). Use official taxi queue or Didi. Metro Line 2 connects to city.

arrival.to: Xiamen Railway Station / Xiamen North Railway Station
arrival.tips: Xiamen North is for high-speed trains. Xiamen Station is closer to center. Book through 12306 app.
High-speed trains are comfortable. scenic route along coast. Book seats on right side for ocean views.

metro: Metro Line 1: Railway Station to Amphitheater (Coastal scenic route)
Metro Line 2: Jiahe Road to Tianzhou Road (connects airport)
Metro Line 3: Handan Road to Songbai Road (connects Xiang'an)
bus: BRT (Bus Rapid Transit): dedicated lanes, faster travel
Tourist Bus 29: links major attractions around Island Ring
Night bus routes available for late-night travel
taxi: Didi app recommended - English interface available
Airport/large hotels have taxi stands with queue system
bike: Mobike, Hello bikes, and other shared bikes widely available
Designated bike lanes on Huandao Road
Electric bikes available for longer distances
Must park at designated spots in some areas

hotels.name: 厦门安达仕酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book direct for best rates. Weekend packages available. Sea-view rooms worth the premium.

hotels.name: 厦门华尔道夫酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book 2+ weeks ahead for best availability. Consider breakfast-inclusive packages.

hotels.name: 厦门康莱德酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request high floor for best views. Hotel buffet is excellent. Good value at weekends.

hotels.name: 厦门香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Club rooms offer good value with benefits. Ask for renovated rooms.

hotels.name: 厦门海景千禧大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Seaview rooms recommended. Book on travel platforms for discounts.

hotels.name: 厦门牡丹万鹏宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for shopping and sightseeing. Check packages for breakfast.

hotels.name: 厦门京闽中心酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Weekend rates significantly lower. Good for business travelers.

hotels.name: 厦门航空金雁酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for early flights. Free airport transfer. Request quiet rooms.

hotels.name: 厦门鹭江宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Book historical wing for unique experience. Walking distance to ferry.

hotels.name: 厦门华侨大厦
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in prime location. Simple but clean rooms. No views but great location.

hotels.name: 厦门七斗星商旅酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Breakfast included makes great value. Good metro connections. Clean and functional.

hotels.name: 厦门如家酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Reliable chain with consistent quality. Book ahead during holidays.

hotels.name: 鼓浪屿红堂酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book early - only hotel on Gulangyu with character. Stay on island for sunrise access.

hotels.name: 鼓浪屿磐石酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location on Gulangyu for first-time visitors. Reserve seaview rooms.

hotels.name: 集美湖酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for exploring Jimei area. Good restaurant. Free parking.

hotels.name: 厦门海沧希尔顿花园酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for business travelers. Clean and comfortable. Free WiFi. Good location.

hotels.name: 厦门国际会议中心酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book sea-view rooms. Great for MICE travelers. Excellent service. Beautiful grounds.

payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in Xiamen
howToUse: Download Alipay app before arrival
Link international credit card (Visa/Mastercard accepted at many merchants)
QR code payment at most merchants, supermarkets, and restaurants
Use for metro, bus, taxis, and rideshare
tips: Nearly all merchants in Xiamen accept Alipay
Blue code (international version) available for foreign cards
Download before arrival as app stores may not have it
Keep some cash as backup in rural areas

payment.description: Second most popular payment method, integrated with social app
howToUse: WeChat app required
Link international card through WeChat Pay
Scan QR code to pay at merchants
Use for small purchases and tipping
tips: English interface available in settings
Most locals prefer WeChat Pay for peer-to-peer
Both Alipay and WeChat work at most merchants
Useful for ordering at some restaurants

payment.description: Still accepted everywhere, essential for local markets and small vendors
howToUse: Exchange at airport bank or ATM
ICBC and Bank of China ATMs widely available
500¥, 100¥, 50¥ notes most useful
Small vendors at markets prefer cash
tips: Keep 100-200¥ in cash for markets
ATMs at Metro stations convenient for cash
Xiamen is mostly cashless - only rural markets need cash
ATMs charge 10-20¥ fee for foreign cards

payment.description: Accepted at hotels, large restaurants, and international chains
howToUse: Visa and Mastercard accepted at upscale venues
May require passport verification
Look for card logos at store entrances
tips: Call your bank before travel to enable international use
Some places add 2-3% foreign transaction fee
Best used at hotels and modern shopping malls
Smaller shops and restaurants often don't accept cards

payment.description: Domestic bank cards - widely accepted across China
howToUse: ATM cards from Chinese banks work everywhere
Can withdraw cash from ICBC, CCB, BOC ATMs
Some merchants prefer domestic cards
tips: If you have Chinese bank account, this works like debit
Lower fees than international cards at ATMs
Ask hotel to help open temporary account if needed

payment.description: Digital wallets like Apple Pay, Samsung Pay increasingly accepted
howToUse: Link international card to Apple Pay/Google Pay
Tap to pay at terminals with contactless symbol
Available at modern stores and hotels
tips: Limited acceptance compared to Alipay/WeChat
Useful if you have foreign card linked
Works at 7-Eleven and some modern chains
Best as backup to Alipay/WeChat

culturalTips.title: Min Nan Dialect Greetings
culturalTips.content: Learn a few闽南话 phrases: 'Li ho' (hello), 'Zuo zai' (thank you), 'Cheh beh' (not good). Locals appreciate the effort even if pronunciation isn't perfect.

culturalTips.title: Seafood Ordering Etiquette
culturalTips.content: At seafood restaurants, prices are often 'per jin' (500g). Confirm total price before ordering. Watch the scale when selecting live seafood. Don't hesitate to negotiate at wet markets.

culturalTips.title: Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Xiamen is famous for tea (mikong tea/功夫茶). When invited to someone's home, expect tea service. Turn the cup with both hands when receiving tea as a sign of respect.

culturalTips.title: Chopstick customs
culturalTips.content: Don't stick chopsticks vertically in rice - it resembles incense for the dead. Don't point with chopsticks. Don't pass food directly from chopstick to chopstick.

culturalTips.title: Gulangyu Island Courtesy
culturalTips.content: Gulangyu is a residential island with many elderly residents. Keep noise levels down, especially near the piano museum and residential areas. No cars are allowed - respect the peaceful atmosphere.

culturalTips.title: Temple Respect
culturalTips.content: At Nanputuo Temple, dress modestly covering shoulders and knees. Remove sunglasses and hats when entering halls. Don't take photos where prohibited. Walk clockwise around Buddhist halls.

culturalTips.title: Photography at Fishing Harbor
culturalTips.content: At Shapowei and fishing harbors, ask before photographing fishermen or their catches. Some may ask for small payment (5-10¥) for photos of them or their boats.

culturalTips.title: Sunrise and Sunset Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Popular sunrise spots (Huandao Road, Gulangyu) get crowded. Don't block others' views or set up tripods for long periods. Be mindful of passing cyclists on Huandao Road.

culturalTips.title: Bargaining in Xiamen
culturalTips.content: Bargaining expected at Zhongshan Road stalls, Zengcuo'an shops, and markets. Start at 40-50% of asking price. Be friendly - it's part of the experience. Walk away if you can't agree.

culturalTips.title: Local Products to Buy
culturalTips.content: Famous Xiamen products:桂圆干 (dried longan), 金门高粱酒 (Kinmen Kaoliang wine), 闽南特产 (Minnan specialties), 茶叶 (tea), 馅饼 (pastries). Buy from reputable shops near Zhongshan Road.

culturalTips.title: Beach Culture
culturalTips.content: Xiamen beaches (Hulhi Beach, Jimei Beach) can get crowded. Don't swim at unguarded beaches. Watch for jellyfish in summer. Clean up after yourself.

culturalTips.title: Respecting Elders
culturalTips.content: In Minnan culture, show respect to elders by addressing them first, offering seats, and not starting eating before older people at the table.

culturalTips.title: University Etiquette
culturalTips.content: When visiting Xiamen University, be respectful of students studying. Don't wander into classroom buildings. The campus is private property - follow all rules.

culturalTips.title: Market haggling
culturalTips.content: At local wet markets, prices are often fixed for locals but negotiable for tourists. Start lower, meet in middle. Bring small bills. Fresh seafood markets are open early morning.

culturalTips.title: Tipping Culture
culturalTips.content: Tipping is not expected or common in Xiamen. High-end restaurants may add service charge. Good service is considered standard - a small tip (10-20¥) for exceptional service at nice restaurants is appreciated.

culturalTips.title: Dancing in Parks
culturalTips.content: Early morning park dancing (广场舞) is popular. Don't interrupt or photograph without permission. Some groups may welcome foreigners to join.

culturalTips.title: Xiamen Snacks to Try
culturalTips.content: Must-try snacks: 土笋冻 (sea worm jelly), 沙茶面 (satay noodles), 烧肉粽 (braised rice dumplings), 花生汤 (peanut soup), 馅饼 (pastries). Buy from local shops in Zhongshan Road or Zengcuo'an.

culturalTips.title: Gambling Culture
culturalTips.content: Mahjong is popular social activity in Xiamen. You may hear mahjong tiles clicking in tea houses or parks. It's a social game, not gambling in the Western sense - tourists are welcome to watch or learn.

culturalTips.title: Taiwan Products
culturalTips.content: Due to proximity to Taiwan, Xiamen has many Taiwan products: Kinmen Kaoliang wine, Taiwan pineapple cakes, dried seafood, and Taiwan-style snacks. Taiwan Commodity City (大嶝小镇) offers duty-free shopping.

culturalTips.title: Teahouse Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Xiamen has many traditional teahouses. When receiving tea, tap two fingers on the table as thanks - this gesture shows respect to the host. Don't pour tea for yourself first; pour for others before yourself.

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门急救中心
emergencyContacts.notes: English operators may be limited. Ask for 'ying yu' (English) operator.

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门市公安局
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist police stations at major attractions and Gulangyu Ferry Terminal

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门消防
emergencyContacts.notes: Also handles rescue operations and accidents

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门大学附属第一医院
emergencyContacts.nameEn: The First Affiliated Hospital of Xiamen University
emergencyContacts.notes: Large public hospital, 24-hour emergency. English interpretation available.

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门市中医院
emergencyContacts.notes: Good for traditional Chinese medicine treatments. Emergency services available.

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门长庚医院
emergencyContacts.notes: Taiwanese-run hospital, English-speaking staff, accepts international insurance

emergencyContacts.name: 新加坡驻厦门总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For Singapore citizens only. Check hours before visiting.

emergencyContacts.name: 马来西亚驻厦门总领事馆
emergencyContacts.address: Xiamen International Bank Building, 50 Lujiang Road

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门出入境管理局
emergencyContacts.notes: For visa extensions, passport issues, and residency permits

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门大学医院
emergencyContacts.notes: On XMU campus, good for minor ailments and health checks

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门眼科中心
emergencyContacts.notes: Specialized eye hospital, 24-hour emergency for eye injuries

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门旅游投诉热线
emergencyContacts.notes: Report travel scams, price disputes, or service issues

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门海上搜救中心
emergencyContacts.notes: For maritime emergencies, drowning, or boat accidents

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门旅游警察
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist police station at Gulangyu Ferry Terminal

emergencyContacts.name: 厦门市人民政府外事办公室
emergencyContacts.notes: Assistance for foreign nationals in Xiamen

quickFacts.language: Mandarin Chinese (Simplified), Minnan dialect widely spoken
quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa (L-tourist, M-business, V-entry). 72-hour transit visa available for some nationalities at XMN.
quickFacts.electricity: 220V/50Hz, Type A/I plugs (same as US/Japan two-pin or China three-pin)
quickFacts.water: Tap water not recommended for drinking. Bottled water widely available (2-5¥).
quickFacts.bestTimeToVisit: March-May for flowers, October-November for weather, avoid July-September for typhoons
